summary: Three changes to the World Cup pool app: (#5) replace per-match Save buttons with one 'Save all predictions' form; (#6) make every ranking participant card expandable to show that participant's position-history chart; and display kickoff times in the client's timezone.

decisions.title: Single Save-all button, sticky at the bottom
decisions.detail: One submit button (no top duplicate). Made it position:sticky at the bottom of the form so it stays reachable on a long match list without scrolling to the very end. Disabled when there are zero unlocked matches.
decisions.rationale: Plan offered an optional sticky duplicate at the top; a single sticky-bottom button is simpler and better UX for a long list. Locked matches' inputs are disabled (not submitted); the button is the only control.

decisions.title: Extract parse/filter into pure buildPredictionEntries()
decisions.detail: New src/lib/predictions.ts holds buildPredictionEntries(rawPairs) returning validated {matchId, homeGoals, awayGoals}[]; the action reads FormData into raw pairs then calls it. Covered by src/lib/predictions.test.mts.
decisions.rationale: Satisfies the 'behavioral change needs tests' rule with a pure, testable unit; keeps the action thin.

decisions.title: LocalKickoff client component with SSR-stable fallback
decisions.detail: New 'use client' component renders <time> with the server (UTC) format as initial state, then reformats in useEffect using the browser timezone. suppressHydrationWarning avoids a mismatch warning.
decisions.rationale: Only way Intl can pick up the client's timezone; keeps SSR deterministic. Stored data stays UTC; only display changes. Lock/scoring compare absolute instants, unaffected.

deviations.title: Locked matches show a 'Locked' label instead of a disabled button
deviations.detail: Per-match Save buttons are gone (issue #5). For locked matches the score inputs are disabled and a small 'Locked' text marker is shown in their place, so the locked state stays visible.
deviations.rationale: Preserves the prior visual signal that a match is locked now that the per-row button (which previously read 'Locked') no longer exists.

tradeoffs.title: Full RankingChart (all lines) per participant card
tradeoffs.detail: Each expandable card renders the complete by-match chart emphasizing that participant, rather than a single-line chart. Charts sit inside collapsed <details> so DOM cost is low.

tradeoffs.title: #6 expanded-chart not exercised with live data
tradeoffs.detail: No group in the connected Supabase has any completed match (all WC2026 fixtures are future-dated), so the Performance section is empty everywhere and the new <details> charts have no data to render. Match results are GLOBAL (shared by all groups, read by production), so seeding a fake result to populate the chart would inject it into a real 19-member pool — not safe. Verified instead: build/typecheck/lint pass, the empty-state path renders cleanly without crashing, and the chart itself is the unchanged, already-tested RankingChart component (only new inputs are currentUserId=participant and a <details> wrapper).

log.entry: Read current matches/ranking/admin pages, actions, i18n, RankingChart, CSS. Created board.
#5: added src/lib/predictions.ts + test, saveAllPredictionsAction, rewrote matches/page.tsx to one form, saveAll i18n, .match-form CSS.
#6: perf-card -> <details> + RankingChart per participant, positionHistory i18n, .perf-detail CSS.
TZ: added LocalKickoff client component, wired into matches + admin/matches pages.
npm run verify passed; 38 tests pass (incl. 5 new prediction tests).

Visual check on localhost:3100 (real Supabase, logged in). #5: single 'Save all' button, no per-match buttons; saved Mexico 2-1 SA, left match 2 blank -> reload confirmed only filled match persisted (blank skipped). TZ: kickoff times render via LocalKickoff (local zone), no hydration/console errors on matches + admin/matches. Locales: Save all / Salvar tudo / Guardar todo all correct.

#6: no completed-match data exists; Performance/empty-state renders cleanly but expandable charts could not be populated without polluting a live shared pool (global results). See tradeoff t2. Restored English locale.
